Industry-Sponsored Projects

MABE — Industry Sponsor

Compact Power Transmission for Washing Machine

CAD & structural simulation lead — cycloidal rotor design, contact-stress & shaft FEA

Aug – Dec 2022
Brief: fit a washing-machine drive into a tight envelope while delivering a 6:1 reduction and meeting torque targets across two operating modes — agitation and high-speed spin.

Cycloidal transmission — SolidWorks assembly, ANSYS shaft torque simulation, and rotor contact stress FEA

After the team benchmarked three architectures (compound, planetary, cycloidal) against a weighted decision matrix, I designed the drive around a single-stage cycloidal reducer — a 6-lobe rotor running against 7 outer rollers — to reach the required 6:1 reduction in one compact disc instead of the multi-stage gear train the other options demanded. I generated the cycloidal rotor geometry in SolidWorks directly from its parametric profile equations and modeled the full assembly (input crank, rotor, output shaft, housing, cover), packaging the gearbox into a ≈⌀4.8 in × 1.5 in envelope.

I then owned the structural validation in ANSYS. I ran contact-stress FEA on the two load-bearing interfaces — rotor-to-outer-rollers (18.8 ksi peak) and rotor-to-internal-pins (38.4 ksi peak, the governing case) — confirming a 1.56 safety factor against normalized 1040 steel. For the output shaft, I compared hexagonal and splined profiles under the 273 lb·in agitation torque, working within the partner's 0.62 in maximum-diameter limit, and ran a full torsional fatigue check (surface, size, load, and stress-concentration factors) that returned fatigue safety factors above 4.6.

✓ 6:1 reduction delivered in a single cycloidal stage, eliminating the multi-stage gear train the alternatives required. Every element cleared its structural acceptance criteria (min 1.56 contact-stress SF, >4.6 shaft fatigue SF); full CAD, manufacturing drawings, process sheets, and at-scale cost estimates delivered to MABE.
SolidWorks Cycloidal Drive Design ANSYS (Contact-Stress FEA) ANSYS (Shaft Fatigue FEA) GD&T / 2D Drawings MATLAB

Metamaterials Lab — Tecnológico de Monterrey

Compliant Gripper for Micro-Assembly

Research intern — design, simulation, fabrication & testing

Aug – Oct 2022
Brief: design a gripper achieving parallel motion for micro-assembly, derived from rigid-body kinematics — then validate it through simulation and physical testing.

Parametric ANSYS simulations — amplification ratio vs joint geometry

FDM-fabricated gripper and custom test bench — physical validation

Translated a rigid-body Scott–Russell mechanism into a compliant design by mapping joint angular displacements to flexure geometry. Ran parametric ANSYS structural simulations varying joint thickness, geometry, and starting angle to characterize their effect on amplification ratio and parasitic Z-axis displacement. Manufactured the gripper and a custom universal testing bench entirely via FDM. Validated simulation results through physical testing and optical motion tracking.

✓ Simulated amplification ratio: 2.4 — experimental: 3.16. Deviation systematically analyzed and attributed to FDM joint geometry tolerances and boundary condition assumptions.
ANSYS (Parametric FEA) SolidWorks MATLAB FDM Manufacturing Motion Tracking

Plásticos Técnicos Mexicanos — Industry Sponsor

Weight Optimization of Industrial Pallet

Structural analysis and design optimization

Feb – Jun 2023
Brief: reduce pallet weight while maintaining certified static and dynamic load capacity; assess manufacturability of the optimized geometry.

Performed iterative design modifications guided by ANSYS static and dynamic FEM results. Built and refined finite element meshes, evaluated stress distributions and deflections under rated load cases, and confirmed compliance with structural acceptance criteria after each design iteration. Used Autodesk Moldflow to verify that the optimized geometry remained injection-moldable — assessing fill, weld lines, and sink risk.

✓ Achieved a 10% weight reduction with no loss of rated load capacity. Moldflow confirmed the geometry is manufacturable via injection molding.
ANSYS (Static + Dynamic FEA) Autodesk Moldflow SolidWorks FE Mesh Refinement

Automotive Transmission Shaft Redesign

Failure analysis and structural redesign

Feb – Jun 2022
Brief: identify the failure mode of an in-service transmission shaft and redesign to eliminate recurrence.

Conducted failure mode identification through structural FEM analysis and classical shaft fatigue mechanics. Evaluated material and geometry changes, validated the redesign through simulation, and delivered technical documentation for the partner.

ANSYS (Structural FEA) SolidWorks Fatigue Mechanics Material Selection

General Electric — Industry Sponsor

Test Bench for Turbine Blade Actuation

Mechanical design, kinematics & simulation

Aug – Oct 2021
Brief: design a compact test bench to replicate and characterize actuation forces in aircraft turbine blade mechanisms.

GE turbine blade test bench — kinematic diagram, SolidWorks base assembly, and final assembly

Designed the bench from a GE-defined brief: developed the kinematic model, validated motion and loads in SolidWorks motion studies, and produced complete technical documentation for the partner. Delivered CAD assemblies and detailed drawings meeting the compact envelope requirement.

SolidWorks Motion Studies Kinematic Analysis Technical Documentation

Research Collaboration

ENES Juriquilla, UNAM — Research Collaboration

Sheet-Metal Tensioned Knee Mechanism

CAD design & assembly — translated a researcher's concept into a fully defined sheet-metal mechanism

CAD assembly
Brief: take a UNAM researcher's concept for a tensioned biomechanical knee and turn it into a complete, manufacturable CAD assembly built around sheet-metal components and a spring-tensioned linkage.

Full mechanism assembly — twin cam profiles linked by a spring-tensioned member, modeled in SolidWorks

Working from the researcher's concept, I modeled the full mechanism as a fully constrained SolidWorks assembly: a pair of opposed cam/pulley profiles coupled through a central tensioned link, packaged with the surrounding sheet-metal structure and fasteners. The work centered on the parts of design I enjoy most, translating a loose idea into defined geometry, mating the assembly so the tensioned motion behaves correctly, and keeping the components manufacturable as sheet metal.

The project concluded at the design and assembly stage, a complete, motion-correct CAD model ready to carry forward into prototyping and validation. It is a close cousin, mechanically, to the spring-tensioned hardware found on stringed instruments, which is part of why I enjoyed it.

✓ Delivered a complete, fully constrained sheet-metal CAD assembly that captured the researcher's concept as motion-correct, manufacturable geometry, ready for a future prototyping phase.
SolidWorks Sheet-Metal Design Assembly & Mechanism Modeling Concept Digitalization

Master's Thesis Research

MADIT Laboratory — UNAM

Direct Polymer Additive Tooling for Low-Volume Cup-Drawing Production

Researcher — design, fabrication, characterization & simulation

2024 – 2026
Research question: can 3D-printed polymer tooling substitute conventional metal dies for low-volume sheet-metal cup-drawing, and what are the actual limits — in geometry accuracy, tool life, and failure modes?

Designing and 3D-printing polymer tools for sheet-metal cup-drawing operations, then running systematic experimental drawing trials to characterize tool performance, geometry accuracy, and failure modes. Material properties are determined experimentally via tensile testing on a Shimadzu universal testing machine, feeding directly into ANSYS structural simulation models. Operating industrial AM equipment at MADIT Lab: Stratasys Fortus 400MC (FDM) and EOS P100 (SLS), including material loading, print parameter selection, and post-processing. The work aims to establish polymer additive tooling as a viable low-cost, fast-lead-time alternative to conventional tooling for low-volume runs.

Expected thesis defense: 2026.

Stratasys Fortus 400MC (FDM) EOS P100 (SLS) ANSYS Structural FEA Tensile Testing (Shimadzu) SolidWorks MATLAB
